name: Container Glass
description:
Resource cycle for soda-lime container glass.

Graph nodes show rolled-up CO₂e (emissions only). Click a node for the step breakdown. The statement table compares Manufacture / Recycle / Reuse / Landfill pathways. Transformation is where ready material becomes the finished article for use.
co2e_basis: Absolute process emissions only (no avoided-burden credits). Illustrative order-of-magnitude from container-glass LCAs — not a certified EPD. Virgin path ≈ raw material + melt; recycle ≈ collect/sort + remelt; reuse ≈ return + wash. All routes emit CO₂e; reuse < recycle < virgin manufacture for the next cycle.

nodes.label: Raw Material
nodes.summary: Quarry sand, limestone & dolomite; produce soda ash; blend batch
nodes.details:
Batch is typically ~70% silica sand, ~15% soda ash, plus limestone/dolomite.

1. Mine and wash silica sand; crush limestone and dolomite.
2. Produce or buy synthetic soda ash (largest upstream GHG in the batch).
3. Dry, weigh, blend; truck batch to the glass plant.

Furnace process CO₂ from carbonates is counted under Manufacture.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.20 kg CO₂e/kg. Mostly synthetic soda ash (~0.10–0.15) plus mining, drying, short-haul transport.

co2e_lines.label: Synthetic soda ash
co2e_lines.note: Largest upstream GHG in the batch

nodes.label: Manufacture
nodes.details:
1. Melt batch in a regenerative or oxy-fuel furnace (~1400–1600 °C).
2. Form on IS machines (blow/press).
3. Anneal in a lehr; inspect and pack empty ware.

Cullet from the Recycle loop rejoins the melt (folded into Recycle → Transformation as remelted ware ready to fill). High cullet cuts fuel and process CO₂ (~3% energy / ~5% CO₂ per 10% cullet).
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.85 kg CO₂e/kg low-cullet: ~0.55–0.65 fuel + ~0.15–0.25 process CO₂ + ~0.05–0.10 forming/annealing. High cullet can cut this toward ~0.4–0.5.

nodes.label: Recycle
nodes.summary: Collect, sort, treat cullet → remelt → rejoin Transformation
nodes.details:
Bottle banks / kerbside → colour sort → cullet plant → furnace remelt → empty ware back into Transformation.

Figures are the emissions of that remelt path itself — not a credit for avoiding virgin production. Remelt still needs fuel; high cullet mainly cuts process CO₂ and some energy vs virgin batch.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.50 kg CO₂e/kg for the remelt path: collect/sort/treat plus high-cullet furnace remelt and forming. Lower than virgin melt, but still a positive emission — not a net climate credit.

nodes.label: Reuse
nodes.summary: Return, wash, inspect — then back into Transformation
nodes.details:
Deposit–return reuse: reverse logistics → industrial wash (hot water, caustic) → inspection → rejoin Transformation.

Each trip still emits (haul + wash energy), but far less than remelting or making new glass. Trip rate and regional loops dominate the climate outcome.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.08 kg CO₂e/kg per reuse trip (return haul + industrial wash). Still emits CO₂e, but less than recycle remelt and far less than virgin manufacture.

nodes.label: Transformation
nodes.summary: Turn ready material into its final form for the end user
nodes.details:
Material arriving from Manufacture, Recycle, or Reuse is transformed into the finished article the end user receives (for glass: rinse/sterilise, fill, cap, label, pack).

Inbound empty ware and outbound finished freight are Deliver edges, not this node.

Product contents’ footprint is out of scope for the glass functional unit.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.04 kg CO₂e/kg glass for rinse/fill/cap line utilities attributed to the container (not the beverage). Varies widely with pasteurisation and line speed.

co2e_lines.label: Line utilities (transform / finish)
co2e_lines.note: Attributed to the finished article, not the contents

nodes.label: Use
nodes.summary: Consumer or venue uses the product; bottle becomes empty again
nodes.details:
Use-phase GHG for the glass itself is negligible. The important choice is the next edge:

• Landfill — material lost
• Recycle — remelt / rejoin Transformation
• Reuse — wash and rejoin Transformation
nodes.co2e_note: ~0 kg CO₂e for the glass object during use. Climate impact is decided by which outgoing branch is taken.

nodes.label: Landfill
nodes.details: Residual-waste collection and burial. Glass is inert in landfill (little methane), but embodied carbon and material are lost permanently.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.02 kg CO₂e/kg collection + landfill ops. Glass is inert (little landfill gas); the climate cost of this route is that the next bottle needs virgin manufacture again.

co2e_lines.label: Collection & landfill ops
co2e_lines.note: Glass is inert; material is lost

edges.co2e_note: ~0.03 kg CO₂e/kg for a typical regional empty-ware haul (~150–300 km).
~0.05 kg CO₂e/kg for a representative filled outbound chain (~200–400 km total).
